commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=f173031b12cf0bcaefa78e27ecc8fe9c756da4a0)
commands.review_summary:
This PR changes compaction chunk timeout handling to return partial summaries, updates preflight compaction-timeout recovery copy to mention the existing timeout setting, and adjusts regression tests.

PR surface: Source +8, Tests +3. Total +11 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main wraps the whole compaction operation in one safety timeout, and the linked issue shows that timeout discards local chunk progress. I did not run a live slow-provider compaction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining blocker is a maintainer decision on the compaction timeout recovery contract, not a safe narrow automation repair.

target.title: fix compaction timeout partial progress
target.body:
## Summary

- Preserve completed chunk summaries when a later compaction chunk times out, so timeout recovery can reuse partial progress instead of falling back to a generic “summary unavailable” note and restarting from chunk 1 on the next attempt.
- Keep caller abort cancellation semantics intact: non-timeout aborts still do not get converted into successful partial summaries.
- Surface compaction timeout details in the preflight failure reply, including the existing `agents.defaults.compaction.timeoutSeconds` knob, while generic non-verbose compaction failures still hide details.

Fixes #92043

## Maintainer-ready notes

- **Why it matters / User impact:** Users with large transcripts can hit the default compaction wall-clock timeout after some chunks already summarized successfully. Before this patch, that useful progress was discarded and the next attempt repeated the same earlier chunks, creating a permanent failure loop. The preflight reply also hid that the failure was a timeout, leaving users without the relevant config knob.
- **Fix classification:** Root-cause reliability fix for compaction timeout progress reuse and timeout failure copy.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High for the local source/runtime surface covered here. The change is XS/S-sized, has RED/GREEN regression coverage for both behavior changes, preserves abort cancellation behavior, and does not alter default timeout values or public config shape.
- **Root cause:** The root source was the compaction summary invariant: once a chunk summary exists, later timeout failures should carry that partial progress to `summarizeWithFallback()`. `summarizeChunks()` instead rethrew timeout errors before attaching `partialSummary`, and `buildPreflightCompactionFailureText()` hid the timeout/config cause at the preflight copy boundary.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** This changes the source owner that enforces the partial-progress invariant, not a downstream display path. Timeout errors now preserve completed chunk summaries only after at least one chunk succeeded; first-chunk failures and non-timeout abort state keep the existing behavior. The copy change stays at the preflight compaction failure formatter, which is the source boundary for revealing sanitized timeout reasons.
- **What did NOT change:** Only timeout handling after completed chunk summaries and preflight timeout failure text changed. Chunk planning, model retry policy, compaction timeout defaults, persistent session storage, provider routing, and unrelated compaction behavior are unchanged and out of scope.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** Source-of-truth assurance: `src/agents/compaction.ts` remains the source-of-truth owner for summary partial-progress behavior, and `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts` remains the source-of-truth owner for preflight agent-run failure copy. No downstream post-processing path, alternate compaction path, schema change, or public config migration was added.
- **Related open PR scan:** `daily-fix init-issue 92043` created a clean issue worktree from `origin/main` and did not report a related-open-PR/public-contract blocker for this issue. The patch only references the existing public config key `agents.defaults.compaction.timeoutSeconds`; it does not introduce or change a config contract.
- **Patch quality notes:** Patch-quality timeout/retry warnings are intentional and bounded: `isTimeoutError(err)` is evaluated before abort handling so timeout-shaped aborts can preserve partial progress, while non-timeout aborts still return cancellation behavior. The regex in the failure formatter only reveals sanitized reasons that mention compaction timeout.
- **Target test file:** `src/agents/compaction-partial-summary.test.ts` and `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-direct-runtime-config.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** Regression test scenario: a two-chunk compaction where chunk 1 succeeds and chunk 2 throws `TimeoutError` must return the chunk 1 summary plus a `[Partial summary: ...]` marker. A preflight compaction timeout must surface the timeout reason and `agents.defaults.compaction.timeoutSeconds` guidance.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The regression tests exercise the production fallback function and production preflight failure routing at the source owner boundary. They also keep the existing abort test in place so timeout recovery cannot convert user cancellation into a successful partial summary.
- **Out of scope:** This PR does not change the default 180s timeout, add persistent checkpoint storage, change the shape/default of `agents.defaults.compaction.timeoutSeconds`, alter compaction chunk planning, or change model/provider retry policy.

## Root Cause

`src/agents/compaction.ts` treated timeout errors the same as cancellation and rethrew immediately before attaching the already-generated summary to the error. `summarizeWithFallback()` therefore had no partial summary to reuse, even when earlier chunks had already completed successfully, so the final fallback became `Context contained ... Summary unavailable due to size limits.`

Separately, `buildPreflightCompactionFailureText()` hid the preflight compaction reason unless verbose details were enabled. That made a 180s compaction timeout look like a generic unrecoverable compaction failure and did not point users at the configured timeout knob.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** A later chunk timeout no longer discards already-generated compaction summary progress; preflight compaction timeout replies now show the timeout reason and `agents.defaults.compaction.timeoutSeconds` guidance.
- **Real environment tested:** Local Linux OpenClaw worktree at `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-92043`, branch `feat/issue-92043`, commit `f173031b12cf0bcaefa78e27ecc8fe9c756da4a0`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/compaction-partial-summary.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-direct-runtime-config.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/compaction-partial-summary.test.ts src/agents/compaction.summarize-fallback.test.ts src/agents/compaction.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-direct-runtime-config.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts
pnpm tsgo:core && pnpm tsgo:core:test
./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/compaction.ts src/agents/compaction-partial-summary.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-direct-runtime-config.test.ts
git diff --check
node --import tsx --input-type=module - < inline preflight timeout proof script
```

- **Evidence after fix:**

```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  6 passed (6)
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  8 passed (8)
[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 66.10s
```

```text
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  82 passed (82)
Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests  27 passed (27)
[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 39.75s
```

```text
$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
All matched files use the correct format.
git diff --check passed
```

```json
{
  "timeoutIncludesReason": true,
  "timeoutIncludesConfigKnob": true,
  "timeoutIncludesRecoveryCommands": true,
  "genericNonVerboseHidesReason": true,
  "timeoutText": "⚠️ Context is too large and auto-compaction could not recover this turn. Reason: Compaction timed out after 180000ms. Try again, use /compact, or use /new to start a fresh session. If this repeats, raise `agents.defaults.compaction.timeoutSeconds` for this session profile.",
  "genericText": "⚠️ Context is too large and auto-compaction could not recover this turn. Try again, use /compact, or use /new to start a fresh session."
}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** A later timeout after chunk 1 completes returns the chunk 1 summary with a `[Partial summary: chunks 1-1 of 2 were summarized...]` marker instead of the generic unavailable summary. Preflight timeout replies now include the sanitized timeout reason, `/compact`, `/new`, and `agents.defaults.compaction.timeoutSeconds`; generic non-timeout preflight failures still hide details unless verbose output is enabled.
- **What was not tested:** No live external model provider, long-running 180s wall-clock gateway run, Desktop session, or external channel transport was exercised. The summary-progress regression is covered through production compaction fallback code with a mocked summarizer timeout seam; the user-facing timeout guidance was verified by directly executing the production formatter locally.

## Regression Test Plan

- `src/agents/compaction-partial-summary.test.ts` now locks the timeout case: once a chunk has completed, a later timeout preserves the latest summary and partial marker.
- The existing abort test remains unchanged in behavior: non-timeout aborts still do not get converted into partial-success summaries.
- `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-direct-runtime-config.test.ts` locks the preflight timeout reply so future changes keep the timeout reason and config knob visible.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** session-state, reliability, user-facing copy, timeout handling.
- **Risk explanation:** The behavior change is limited to compaction timeout errors after at least one chunk has already produced a summary. First-chunk failures still fall through to the existing generic fallback, and non-timeout aborts still propagate as cancellation.
- **Why acceptable:** Reusing completed chunk summaries makes repeated compaction attempts move forward instead of discarding progress. The copy change only reveals timeout reasons for compaction timeout failures; generic non-verbose compaction errors remain redacted.
- **Out-of-scope boundary:** This PR does not change the default 180s timeout, add persistent checkpoint storage, alter compaction chunk planning, or change model/provider retry policy.
target.author: mushuiyu886

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
